commands.review_summary:
The PR carries Claude stream-json tool-use counts through CLI output parsing and skips `empty_response` fallback when an empty Claude result followed tool use.

PR surface: Source +30, Tests +125. Total +155 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source-reproducible: the linked production logs plus current main show an empty Claude result becomes `output.text === ""`, which the runner converts into `empty_response`. I did not run a live Telegram plus Claude CLI reproduction in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Human review should wait on contributor real behavior proof and maintainer confidence in the message-delivery fallback boundary; automation cannot prove the contributor's live setup for them.

target.title: [codex] Fix Claude tool-only empty response fallback
target.body:
Fixes #91302.

## What changed

- Track Claude CLI tool-use events in JSONL output parsing.
- Preserve tool-use metadata on Claude turns that finish with an empty `result`.
- Avoid `empty_response` failover when a CLI turn has tool calls but no final assistant text.

## Why

Claude can complete a turn by calling a delivery tool, such as `message`, and then emit `result: ""`. The runner previously treated that empty text as a failed response, which could trigger fallback behavior and duplicate externally delivered replies.

## Validation

```sh
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/cli-output.test.ts src/agents/cli-runner.reliability.test.ts
```
target.author: premsenareddy
